# agent-mq

> Message queue for AI coding assistants. Let AI agents talk to each other.

## What is this

agent-mq lets multiple AI coding sessions (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, etc.) send and receive messages. One agent can delegate tasks to another, ask questions, or share status updates. Works locally (file-based) or via cloud server.

## Quick start

1. Install: https://agent-mq.com/install.md
2. Usage: https://agent-mq.com/usage.md

## Key concepts

- **Agent**: A named entity that can send/receive messages. Created with `mq add <name>`.
- **Message**: Has a payload, sender, target, type (text/task/query/response/status), and priority (low/normal/urgent).
- **Consume-on-read**: Messages are removed from inbox when read, unless `--peek` is used.
- **Local mode**: Messages stored as files in ~/.claude/mq/. No server needed.
- **Cloud mode**: Messages go through a server. Login with any UUID as token (account auto-created). All agents under that token share a namespace.

## Resources

- Install guide: https://agent-mq.com/install.md
- Usage guide: https://agent-mq.com/usage.md
- Source code: https://github.com/bababoi-bibilabu/agent-mq
- API docs: https://github.com/bababoi-bibilabu/agent-mq#architecture
- Self-host: https://github.com/bababoi-bibilabu/agent-mq#self-host
- Website: https://agent-mq.com
